headline: Cabinet decisions remain classified under Official Secrets Act
context: All cabinet meeting minutes and decisions remain permanently classified under the Official Secrets Act 1972, with no declassification timeline or public disclosure mechanism.

cards.big: Every cabinet decision in Malaysian history is permanently classified as secret
cards.sub: No declassification timeline exists — even 50-year-old decisions remain hidden

cards.big: OSA 1972 Section 2A makes cabinet papers automatically 'top secret'
cards.sub: Penalty for disclosure is up to life imprisonment — harsher than some murder sentences

cards.big: UK declassifies cabinet papers after 20 years under Public Records Act
cards.sub: Historians and citizens can examine government reasoning from past decades

cards.big: Collective cabinet responsibility doctrine used to block all information requests
cards.sub: Ministers cannot disclose even their own positions on past policy debates

cards.big: Permanent secrecy doesn't protect national security — it protects politicians from accountability
Should Malaysia adopt a 25-year declassification rule for cabinet records?
